data.title: Integer Holdings Non-GAAP EPS of $1.76 beats by $0.06, revenue of $472.06M beats by $9.34M
data.description:
• Integer Holdings press release (ITGR): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.76 beats by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $472.06M (+5.0% Y/Y) beats by $9.34M.
  • Cardio & Vascular sales increased 11% to $284 million in the fourth quarter 2025 compared to fourth quarter 2024, driven by the Precision Coating and VSi Parylene acquisitions, and strong demand in Neurovascular. Full year sales increased 17% year-over-year to $1.107 billion, driven by strong growth from new product ramps in Electrophysiology, contributions from acquisitions, and strong demand in Neurovascular.
  • FY26 sales consensus of $1.84B, EPS consensus of $6.30

  • 2026 Outlook(a)

data.title: Upstart repurchases $100M of shares
data.description:
• Upstart Holdings (UPST) said it purchased $100M of common stock over the last four trading days pursuant to a $400M share repurchase program previously authorized by its board.
  • Upstart purchased a total of 3,193,294 shares of common stock at an average price of $31.31 per share between February 12, 2026, and February 18, 2026.
  • After these repurchases, there is $122M remaining under the program.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Dell in focus as Evercore adds to Tactical Outperform list
data.description:
Dell Technologies (DELL) was in focus on Thursday as investment firm Evercore added the technology giant to its Tactical Outperform list ahead of its next quarterly results, set to be released on Feb. 26.

Shares rose 1.2% in premarket trading.

"We expect DELL to print upside to current rev/EPS expectations of $31.4B/3.52 given strong [near-term] demand trends across traditional hardware (PCs/servers) and AI compute," analyst Amit Daryanani wrote in a note to clients. "With concerns around memory inflation, expect DELL to have benefited from a demand pull-in across PCs and traditional servers as customers will have looked to get ahead of ASP increases. In ISG, AI server momentum will continue to drive growth, with DELL exiting FQ3 with AI orders of $12.3B and backlog of $18.4B, while also guiding FY26 AI server revs of $25B—implying a step up to $9B+ in the Jan-qtr AI revenues. In CSG, early IDC data suggests Dell gained ~100bps of share in Q4, marking its first share gain in over three years."

Daryanani, who maintained his Outperform rating on Dell, lowered his price target to $160 from $180, due in part to the recent multiple compression in tech stocks.

Delving deeper, the analyst said he expects some gross margin pressure as Dell works through pricing issues, though that should rebound in short order.  "While DELL will work to mitigate memory headwinds through internal levers (LTAs, reconfigurations), some of the cost will inevitably be passed onto end-customers,"  Daryanani explained.

All told, he expects Dell to leave the quarter with momentum from PCs and servers, especially AI servers. "The debate will then shift to margin durability and EPS growth in FY27, though we see multiple ways for DELL to reach double-digit EPS growth, including accelerated share repurchases + upside to AI server revenues, which’ll become a meaningful part of total [revenues]," the analyst added.

A consensus of analysts expects Dell to earn an adjusted $3.52 per share on $31.65B in revenue for the coming quarter.

data.title: Nestlé may follow Unilever in unloading its ice cream business
data.description:
Nestlé S.A. (NSRFY) (NSRGF) is actively moving to exit most or all of its remaining ice cream operations, according to reports. The strategic review and advanced sale talks appear to be centered on the company's Froneri joint venture. 

The Switzerland-based company is considering a smaller presence in the ice cream category, including options to reduce its stake in Froneri or sell remaining wholly owned ice cream businesses into that structure. The businesses under review include local ice cream operations that were not previously contributed to Froneri, which already controls global brands such as Häagen-Dazs and Mövenpick in many markets. Nestlé has indicated that the sale strategy is part of an effort to simplify its portfolio and sharpen its focus on the core pillars of coffee, pet care, nutrition, and food and snacks. 

Nestlé (NSRFY) (NSRGF) has already transferred large parts of its ice cream business into Froneri over the past decade, including its European operations in 2016 and its U.S. ice cream business in a roughly $4B deal announced in 2019, making Froneri one of the largest global ice cream players. While Nestlé (NSRFY) (NSRGF) has confirmed the strategic intent and the advanced nature of discussions with Froneri, no binding transaction terms, valuation, or precise timing have been disclosed publicly.

Unilever (UL) also exited the ice cream business recently with a spinoff of the division as a standalone listed company. In March 2024, Unilever announced plans to separate its global ice cream portfolio, including brands such as Magnum, Ben & Jerry’s, Cornetto, and Wall’s, through a demerger designed to simplify the group and focus on higher-growth beauty, personal care, home care, and nutrition businesses. The separation was completed in December 2025, creating The Magnum Ice Cream Company (MICC), which now trades independently in Amsterdam, London, and New York, with Unilever (UL) retaining a stake of under 20% to be sold down over time.

Shares of Nestlé S.A. (NSRFY) (NSRGF) are up 2.5% in Zurich trading following the company's earnings report released earlier in the day.

data.title: Alight Non-GAAP EPS of $0.18 misses by $0.05, revenue of $653M misses by $2.29M
data.description:
• Alight press release (ALIT): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.18 misses by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $653M (-4.0% Y/Y) misses by $2.29M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $178 million compared to the prior year period of $217 million.
  • Adjusted gross profit of $272 million and adjusted gross profit margin of 41.7%, compared to $300 million and 44.1% in the prior year period, respectively.
